concept.headline: Modules provide namespacing and shared behavior reuse.
concept.explanation: In Crystal, modules can define constants and methods. Classes can include modules to gain instance methods without inheritance duplication.
concept.syntax:
module Greeter
  def greet
    "Hello!"
  end
end

class Bot
  include Greeter
end

puts Bot.new.greet

demo.intro: Mix a module into a class and call the injected method.
demo.code:
module Greeter
  def greet
    "Hello!"
  end
end

class Bot
  include Greeter
end

puts Bot.new.greet

explanation.misconception: Method resolution checks class then included modules

prediction.question: If class Car includes module Startable with method start returning "go", what does puts Car.new.start print?
prediction.explanation: include adds module instance methods to class instances, so output is go.

explanation.afterDemo: Modules are key to Crystal architecture: they keep behavior reusable and avoid deep inheritance chains.
keyTakeaways: Mixins keep code DRY and composable.

practice.prompt: Create module Taggable with method tag returning "[ok]". Include it in class Job and print Job.new.tag.
practice.starterCode:
# module Taggable
# class Job include Taggable
# print tag
practice.solutionCode:
module Taggable
  def tag
    "[ok]"
  end
end

class Job
  include Taggable
end

puts Job.new.tag
practice.hint: Include the module inside class Job.

feedback.successMessage: Excellent. Module behavior was mixed in correctly.

summary.title: Modules — Complete
takeaways: include gives classes module instance methods.
